statement: I run a neighborhood restaurant and want to understand what keeps first-time guests from becoming regulars.
notes: Focus on operational, menu, and service pain points that influence repeat visits.

statement: I run a casual takeout restaurant and want to know which customer pain points most hurt perceived speed and reliability.
notes: Look for signals tied to pickup flow, order accuracy, packaging, and wait times.

statement: I run a premium casual restaurant and need to understand when guests feel the experience is not worth the price.
notes: Look for value breakdowns tied to portions, service, ambiance, and consistency.

statement: I run a date-night restaurant and want to know which issues most damage the feeling of a special evening.
notes: Prioritize ambiance, pacing, reservation handling, noise, and service recovery.

statement: I run a brunch spot and want to understand what frustrates guests during busy weekend visits.
notes: Look at waits, line management, seating, communication, and kitchen throughput.

statement: I run a family-friendly restaurant and want to know what makes parents feel the experience is stressful instead of easy.
notes: Focus on seating, kids' menu clarity, pacing, noise, and staff accommodation.

statement: I run a restaurant serving guests with dietary restrictions and want to understand what most affects trust and confidence.
notes: Look for signals around allergy handling, labeling, staff knowledge, and cross-contamination fears.

statement: I run a restaurant and want to understand which mistakes customers are willing to forgive versus which ones permanently damage trust.
notes: Focus on recovery quality after delays, wrong orders, rude service, or billing issues.

statement: I run a late-night restaurant and want to know what makes the experience feel unreliable after peak evening hours.
notes: Look for patterns tied to staffing, cleanliness, item availability, and speed late in the night.

statement: I run an independent local restaurant and want to understand why some guests try us once but do not feel compelled to recommend us.
notes: Look for issues tied to distinctiveness, signature items, hospitality, and memorability.
